what_changed: Rust struct literals now lower to Seq("rust_struct_expression") instead of an untagged Seq.
The new Rust-only SequenceSurface::RustStructExpression contract is exact-tree-safe but not a collection, map, membership receiver, map-entry list, or imported literal proof.

This lets functions such as Interner::new, whose body combines an exact-safe Rust struct literal with import-backed associated constructors, enter exact admission without treating raw associated-function spelling as type identity.
Untagged Seq, Go composite literals, Rust patterns, collection/map domains, and receiver-domain contracts remain fail-closed unless they carry their own matching evidence.

notes: The two imported-member target-present primary representatives exposed by #578 disappear from the primary callee surface.
The source-surface bucket also drops because Rust struct literals no longer remain untagged exact-closed sequences.
The value-fingerprint-too-small bucket increases from 6 to 13: these units are now strict-exact-safe, but their value fingerprints remain below the exact-claim substance floor.
One receiver-domain unit becomes newly visible after the struct-expression surface opens its enclosing exact path; it remains in a narrower named bucket.
decision.follow_up: Continue with receiver-domain evidence for Rust self/field receivers and remaining member-call proof without opening builder chains or raw receiver spelling.
